question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: MempoolTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the HPA configuration alongside a flood of /mempool/transaction requests to trigger rapid scale-up/scale-down cycles, causing repeated node restarts that keep >= 30% of nodes unavailable at any given time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a JSON body that triggers maximum CPU utilization (80% threshold in HPA) through expensive parsing of types.NetworkRequest, causing the HPA to scale up to maxReplicas: 10 and then immediately crash each new node with the same flood, effectively keeping >= 30% of nodes in a crash-restart loop?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a global request timeout in mempoolAPIService to send slow-loris style requests to the /mempool endpoint, where the HTTP headers are sent very slowly, holding goroutines open indefinitely and exhausting the goroutine pool on >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: MempoolTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a global request timeout in mempoolAPIService to send slow-loris style requests to the /mempool/transaction endpoint, holding goroutines open indefinitely and exhausting the goroutine pool on >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a JSON body containing a very large number of unique keys in a metadata map to the /mempool endpoint, exploiting the Go map allocation overhead during SDK parsing of types.NetworkRequest, to cause GC pressure and latency spikes that degrade >= 30% of nodes?
